insight: In the FIFA World Cup 2026, Qatar's performance has shown a notable trend during hydration breaks, where they have successfully gained momentum in both instances, resulting in a mean increase of 0.150 points per minute. This suggests that the breaks have given Qatar an opportunity to regroup and refocus, allowing them to come back stronger and more determined. Notably, in the two instances where Qatar was not pressing before the break, they were able to maintain this positive momentum, averaging an increase of 0.150 points per minute, indicating that the breaks have been a crucial factor in their ability to stay competitive. Overall, the data implies that Qatar has effectively utilized the hydration breaks to their advantage, using the time to recharge and adjust their strategy to gain an edge over their opponents.
